editor_note: This reads as native, well-edited Arabic technical prose written by someone who knows the domain, not machine translation. Core terminology is correct and consistent throughout: البدائيات (primitives), المرصود (observable), القيمة المتوقعة (expectation value), الحالة الذاتية/القيمة الذاتية (eigenstate/eigenvalue), هرمتي (Hermitian), التراكب/التشابك (superposition/entanglement), and the field-conventional transliterations الهاملتوني (Hamiltonian) and الأنساتز (ansatz) are each used one consistent way; قطرنة is used consistently for "diagonalize" and قمع الأخطاء/تخفيف الأخطاء cleanly distinguish suppression vs mitigation. Meaning is faithful in every load-bearing passage I checked against the source, including the subtle ones: the min/max inversion in the max-cut mapping ("natural tendency is to find minima... so instead of maximizing C(z) we minimize"), the bias/variance tradeoff caveat ("as we reduce bias, variance increases... overhead beyond reducing bias, so not done by default"), the "if you already had the eigenvalues there would be no need for a quantum computer" pedagogical point, the sparse-vs-dense efficiency conditions, and the hedged "with great difficulty one can see error mitigation improves results slightly" (بصعوبة كبيرة... يحسّن النتائج قليلاً) — no softening, strengthening, inverted condition, swapped quantity, or dropped caveat. Informal register holds throughout (كنت تتساءل، دعنا، نشجعك) with no formal antum slips. The only thing I noticed is a trivial orthographic typo قطرنةه for قطرنته at one spot, which is mechanical and does not affect meaning; not counted as an editorial issue.

The bulk of this file is genuinely well-written, native technical Arabic — the introduction, variational-principle derivation, ansatz/optimizer sections and the COBYLA "mountain with eyes closed" analogy all read fluently and stay meaning-faithful, and core terms like طاقة الحالة الأساسية (ground state energy), كرة بلوخ (Bloch sphere), دالة الموجة التجريبية (trial wave function) and المؤثر الرصدي (observable) are correct. But there is one true semantic inversion that misleads a chemistry learner: at the reaction-energy section the English "reactions either absorb energy (endothermic) or release energy (exothermic)" is rendered "التفاعلات إما تمتص الطاقة (طاردة للحرارة) أو تطلق الطاقة (ماصة للحرارة)" — the parenthetical labels are swapped, so "absorb" is tagged exothermic (طاردة للحرارة) and "release" is tagged endothermic (ماصة للحرارة), the exact reverse of the correct definitions. Separately, the core term "variational" is rendered three different ways within this one file — التباديلي in the title and early sections, التبايني at line 877, and التبايينية/التباينية in the conclusion and review questions — a ≥3-way within-file inconsistency on a central term. The endothermic/exothermic swap alone is FAIL-grade because it teaches a false fact; the variational inconsistency compounds it.

This is a short, marketing-flavored closing lesson (IBM Quantum Safe Explorer/Advisor/Remediator) and the Arabic is meaning-faithful throughout: no inverted conditions, no swapped quantities, no dropped caveats, and no hallucinated sentences. Register is correctly informal (تعلّمت، بنيتك، اكتشف، استكشف، حوِّلها). "Cryptographic landscape" is rendered consistently as "بنيتك التشفيرية," "static code analysis" → "التحليل الاستاتيكي للكود," and the CBOM/SBOM brand strings are sensibly kept in English. Two small terminology imprecisions keep this just below a clean PASS but they do not mislead a learner: "object code" is rendered "كود الأجهزة" (literally "hardware code") where the precise sense is compiled/object code ("الكود الكائني/الهدف"), and "Advisor correlates CBOMs" becomes "يرتبط Advisor بين CBOMs," which reads as "Advisor links/relates between CBOMs" rather than the cleaner "يربط/يقرن" for correlate. Also "all cryptographically relevant artifacts" is loosened to "المنتجات ذات الصلة" (products), where "العناصر/الأدلة" would be more accurate, but the gist survives. None of these invert or distort the underlying claim; they are isolated word-choice slips in otherwise natural technical prose.

This is a faithful, meaning-accurate translation that reads as native Czech technical prose. Core terminology is correct and consistent: "qubit/qubity", "hradlo" for gate, "obvod" for circuit, "provázání" for entanglement (glossed once as "(entanglováno)"), "superpozice", and the field-conventional English kept for "backend", "transpiler", "Sampler", "little-endian" and "Qiskit patterns". I read the meaning-critical passages paragraph-against-paragraph, with special attention to the last 40%: the measurement-collapse explanation ("kolaps stavu ... s pravděpodobností rovnou |c_i|²"), the right-to-left matrix subtlety ("operátor nejblíže vektoru stavu působí jako první"), the Toffoli iff ("tehdy a jen tehdy"), and crucially the deliberately-FALSE true/false quiz items (Q2 "stav jednoho qubitu je nezávislý na stavu druhého", Q4 "na úplně levou pozici", Q8 "$2N$") — all preserved exactly rather than silently "corrected", which is the right call for teaching material. The only blemishes are isolated grammatical-agreement/naturalness slips that never mislead: "Kombinováním bitů umožňuješ uložit" (awkward reflexive where "ti umožňuje" is wanted), a gender wobble on "half-adder" ("sčítačky" fem vs "sčítače" masc across the file), and "dalšího nejvýznamnějšího číslice" (gender mismatch). editor_note: This reads like genuine native technical Spanish, not MT: the prose is fluent and idiomatic ("Se trata de un circuito de teleportación", "¡De hecho, muchas cosas podrían salir mal!", "todo este trabajo adicional nos beneficiará"), and the informal first-person-plural course register is held consistently ("Comenzamos", "consideremos", "necesitamos", "podemos hacernos"). Domain terminology is correct and internally consistent: "puerta(s)" for gate, "qubits lógicos/físicos", "código corrector de errores cuánticos", "modelo de ruido estocástico independiente", "punto de equilibrio" for break-even, and "gadgets" sensibly kept in English (matching the source's own *gadgets*). The single real meaning issue is one dropped caveat in the final section: the English line 76 says the 9-qubit Shor code makes things worse "for independent errors" above the break-even point, and the Spanish (line 73) drops the qualifier "para errores independientes" entirely — "el código de Shor de 9 qubits empeora las cosas si la probabilidad de error... está por encima del punto de equilibrio." Because this qualifier scopes a comparative aside rather than the lesson's central claim (and the surrounding noise-model discussion is fully faithful), it is an isolated omission that mildly under-specifies the analogy rather than misleading the learner about the main point — hence MINOR, not FAIL.

editor_note: This is faithful, well-edited French technical prose that teaches as clearly as the English. Core terminology is correct and consistent throughout: "tolérance aux fautes", "code correcteur d'erreurs", "qubits logiques/physiques", "décohérence", "modèle de bruit stochastique indépendant", "seuil" for threshold, "point d'équilibre" for break-even point, and "couplage parfait" for the graph-theory "perfect matching" algorithm — all conventional choices. I checked the meaning-critical passages closely and all hold: the classical-computations-are-perfect exception (l.26), the "aucune corrélation" claim (l.39), the subtle relaxation where "seules les positions des erreurs sont indépendantes" but the errors themselves may be correlated (l.48), the "nature... pas malveillante, n'essaie pas intentionnellement de ruiner" negation (l.45), the five-physical-qubits / 5-qubit-code quantities (l.65), and the final threshold argument with the 9-qubit Shor code aggravating things when error probability "dépasse le point d'équilibre" (l.74). Two trivially stiff calques worth noting but not flagging as defects: "soi-disant gadgets" (l.63) carries a faint pejorative tone where the English "so-called" is neutral-introductory ("dits/appelés gadgets" would read cleaner), and "difficile à raisonner" (l.34) for "difficult to reason about". 전체적으로 충실도(fidelity), 밀도 행렬, 양의 준정부호(positive semidefinite), 대각합 노름(trace norm), 고유값/고유벡터, 유니터리, 정규직교 기저, 정화(purification), 분광 분해, 비파괴 측정 등 핵심 도메인 용어가 한국어 관용 표기로 정확하고 일관되게 쓰였고, 해요체/합니다체 설명 톤도 교재로서 무리 없이 영어 원문의 직관과 강조를 잘 전달합니다. 다만 부드러운 측정 보조정리 절(L159)에서 영어 원문의 "is greater than 1−ε"를 "1보다 크다"로 옮겨 핵심 경계(bound)에서 "−ε"가 누락되었는데, 충실도는 항상 1 이하이므로 이 문장은 수학적으로 모순이며 학습자를 오도할 수 있습니다(바로 아래 수식이 올바르게 `> 1-ε`를 보여 주어 부분적으로 자가 교정됨). 또한 해당 문장은 "ρ와 ... 상태 사이에서 0의 결과가 조건부일 때"처럼 영어 어순을 따라 어색하게 재배열되어 가독성이 떨어집니다. 의미 결함이 이 한 곳에 국한되고 인접 수식이 올바른 경계를 보여 주므로 FAIL이 아닌 MINOR로 판정합니다. (주입된 설문 블록 L323의 "개선를 도와해 주세요" 같은 문법 오타는 핵심 교육 내용이 아니라 평가 대상에서 제외.)

editor_note: Meaning is faithful throughout — no inverted conditions, swapped quantities, dropped caveats, or hallucinations. The prose meaning is overwhelmingly faithful — physics intuition, caveats, and quantities all survive intact, and most paragraphs read as competent native technical Malay. However the file splits cleanly into two terminology registers that collide, crossing the ≥3-inconsistency FAIL bar. "observable" is "boleh cerap" through the first ~70% (lines 174, 297, 315, 437) but becomes "pemboleh ukur" in the Konsep utama section (lines 960, 964, 965); "operator" is "operator" throughout the body (122, 297, 437, 494, 508) but switches to "pengendali" in the closing sections (962-966, 987); "commute/do not commute" is "penukaran" early (494, 506) but "bertukar tertib" at the end (961, 965, 972); "eigenstate" is "keadaan eigen" (122, 290, 293) vs "eigenkeadaan" (778, 881); and the head term itself flips spelling from "ketidakpastian" (used ~30 times) to "ketakpastian" from line 692 onward. Compounding this is a genuine mistranslation at line 953: the English "Pick any observables you like" becomes "Pilih mana-mana pemerhati yang anda suka" — "pemerhati" means "observer/onlooker", not "observable". The injected "Baca soalan di bawah... klik segi tiga" lines and the Accordion→details conversion are expected doQumentation scaffolding, not defects. Register is mostly standard "anda" but slips to "Anda"/"Kamu" inconsistently (e.g. lines 47, 122, 176, 1017) — that alone is MINOR, but it reinforces the two-pass feel. The meaning never inverts and no learner would be misled about the physics; the FAIL is driven by the systematic body-vs-tail terminology fork plus the pemerhati error.

editor_note: This is faithful, well-edited Thai technical prose that teaches as clearly as the English. I read all three sections paragraph-against-paragraph with special attention to the negations and quantifiers that drive this lesson, and every load-bearing claim survives: the no-cloning statement keeps "ไม่มีสถานะควอนตัม... สำหรับทุกสถานะ" (no state exists, for every state), the "perfect vs approximate" caveat is preserved ("การโคลน *สมบูรณ์แบบ* เป็นไปไม่ได้ — แต่... ความแม่นยำที่จำกัด"), the cNOT example correctly notes it would NOT clone |+⟩ ("จะไม่ประสบความสำเร็จ"), and the discrimination proof keeps the non-orthogonal⇒indistinguishable condition and its logical converse ("ถ้าเรามีวิธีแยกแยะ... แล้วพวกมันต้องตั้งฉากกัน"). Terminology is correct and internally consistent: เฟสสากล / เฟสสัมพัทธ์ for global/relative phase, การวัดฐานมาตรฐาน for standard basis measurement, ตั้งฉาก for orthogonal, and field-conventional terms (qubit, gate, circuit, unitary, inner/tensor product, stochastic matrix, workspace) are sensibly kept in English throughout. Register is uniformly casual with no polite particles. The only blemish is the slightly calqued "เช่นนั้น" rendering "such that" in the setup paragraph, where a native writer would more likely use "โดยที่" — but it is grammatical and does not mislead, so it does not move the verdict.

examples.source: ...for some real number θ, such that |φ⟩ = α|ψ⟩.
examples.translation: สำหรับจำนวนจริงบางค่า θ, เช่นนั้น |φ⟩ = α|ψ⟩.
examples.suggested: ...สำหรับจำนวนจริง θ บางค่า โดยที่ |φ⟩ = α|ψ⟩.
examples.why: 'เช่นนั้น' is a calqued rendering of 'such that' that reads slightly stiff; native technical Thai uses 'โดยที่'. Meaning is intact, so this is a stylistic nit only, not a defect.

editor_note: This reads as competent, meaning-faithful Thai technical prose written for a learner, not raw MT. Core domain terms are kept in English per the established Qiskit-Thai convention and used consistently throughout (ground state energy, Hamiltonian, ansatz/ansätze, basis set, qubit, observable, superposition, entanglement, transpilation, COBYLA), while genuinely-Thai renderings like ค่าคาดหวัง for "expectation value", หลักการ variational, and พลังงานของปฏิกิริยา for "reaction energy" are correct and stable. I checked every drift-prone passage and the meaning is intact: the variational inequality direction (เท่ากับหรือสูงกว่า ... เสมอ, ≥) is preserved; the subtle "VQE gives an upper bound yet a noisy measurement can land slightly lower" paradox at the H2 result is rendered faithfully (ให้ขอบเขตบน ... พลังงานที่วัดได้ต่ำกว่า ... เล็กน้อย); the deliberately-false True/False items (#3 "exactly solve the Schrödinger equation", #5 "does not impact the quality") are translated verbatim rather than silently "corrected"; and the ansatz-2-vs-3 conditional and the barren-plateau gradient pairing in the appendix are not swapped. The register is consistently informal (คุณ, no ครับ/ค่ะ). Only two trivial translationese spots, neither misleading: "ต่อสู้ดิ้นรน" for "struggle with" is a touch melodramatic, and "รู้สึกอิสระที่จะ" calques "feel free to".

examples.source: feel free to comment or uncomment lines / feel free to use a simulator
examples.translation: รู้สึกอิสระที่จะ comment หรือ uncomment ... / รู้สึกอิสระที่จะใช้ simulator
examples.suggested: สามารถ comment หรือ uncomment ... ได้ตามต้องการ / จะใช้ simulator ก็ได้
examples.why: 'รู้สึกอิสระที่จะ' is a literal calque of English 'feel free to'; a native technical writer would phrase it with ...ได้ตามต้องการ. Meaning is fully intact, so this is cosmetic only.

examples.source: tackling problems classical computers struggle with
examples.translation: จัดการกับปัญหาที่คอมพิวเตอร์คลาสสิกต่อสู้ดิ้นรน
examples.suggested: จัดการกับปัญหาที่คอมพิวเตอร์คลาสสิกทำได้ยาก
examples.why: 'ต่อสู้ดิ้นรน' (struggle/strive desperately) is slightly more dramatic than the neutral English 'struggle with'; ทำได้ยาก reads more naturally in technical prose. Does not change meaning.

editor_note: This reads as competent technical Thai written in the field's standard hybrid convention: general verbs/structure in Thai (ปรับเปลี่ยน, ความยาวพันธะ, ลู่เข้า "converged", สัญญาณรบกวน for noise) while keeping the established English domain terms inline (Hamiltonian, ansatz, cost function, backend, transpile, estimator, error mitigation, ground state, interatomic distance). That terminology is used consistently within the file and matches how a Thai quantum engineer would actually write — not over-translated, not leaked. Meaning is faithful end-to-end: I checked every meaning-sensitive passage including the last 40%, e.g. the negated claim at L584 ("not likely to improve... since all optimizations converged in fewer than the maximum iterations" → "ไม่น่าจะปรับปรุง... เนื่องจาก...ลู่เข้า...ในจำนวนการวนซ้ำน้อยกว่าสูงสุด") and the comparison at L598 (-1.17 Hartrees, real hardware closer than simulator, error mitigation only on real hardware) are all preserved with correct polarity and quantities. The informal register holds (no ครับ/ค่ะ). The only soft spots are minor flattenings like "play the role of our interatomic distance" rendered as plain "เพื่อเป็น interatomic distance" — stylistic, not misleading. No semantic drift, dropped caveats, or hallucinated assertions.

examples.source: we vary the geometry of a simple molecule, minimizing the energy at each step
examples.translation: เราปรับเปลี่ยน geometry ของโมเลกุลอย่างง่าย โดยลดพลังงานในแต่ละขั้นตอน
examples.why: Representative of the consistent hybrid convention: English domain noun (geometry) kept inline, action verbs and structure in idiomatic Thai. Faithful and natural.

examples.source: increasing the number of optimization steps is not likely to improve the results... since all optimizations actually converged to the required tolerance in fewer than the maximum number of iterations
examples.translation: การเพิ่มจำนวนขั้นตอนการ optimization เพียงอย่างเดียวไม่น่าจะปรับปรุงผลลัพธ์... เนื่องจากการ optimization ทั้งหมดลู่เข้าสู่ tolerance ที่ต้องการในจำนวนการวนซ้ำน้อยกว่าสูงสุดจริง ๆ แล้ว
examples.why: Checked for inverted negation/swapped reasoning: the hedge ('not likely') and the causal clause ('converged in fewer than max iterations') are both preserved correctly. No drift — cited as a verified non-issue in the highest-risk passage.

editor_note: This is a faithful, meaning-preserving translation that teaches as clearly as the English, with correct and consistent domain terminology — qubit, gate, circuit, kernel, feature map, hyperplane, support vector machine, perceptron, activation function, decision boundary, and primal/dual formulation are all kept in English per field convention and never drift across the file. The informal register holds throughout (rerepasoin natin, tayo, mo, no po/opo). The load-bearing pedagogical passages are all intact: the SVM margin/support-vector definition (line 42), the negated kernel-trick caveat on line 63 (the critical "__hindi__" is preserved), the dual-form rationale (line 91), the perceptron non-linearity argument (line 127), and the Pérez-Salinas single-qubit quote (line 172) all match the source. The only blemishes are isolated word-choice slips, not meaning errors: on line 78 "ang mataas na dimensionality ay nagpapagaling ng mga inner product na mas mahal sa computational" uses "nagpapagaling" (heals/makes-better), the wrong verb for "makes more computationally expensive" — but the trailing clause "na mas mahal sa computational" rescues the actual cost claim so the learner is not misled; and on line 101 "feature map na nagpapalit ng linearly separable" awkwardly renders "makes linearly separable." Neither inverts a condition, swaps a quantity, or drops a caveat, so these are naturalness slips rather than semantic drift.

examples.source: In such cases, the high-dimensionality makes inner products more computationally expensive.
examples.translation: Sa mga ganitong kaso, ang mataas na dimensionality ay nagpapagaling ng mga inner product na mas mahal sa computational.
examples.suggested: Sa mga ganitong kaso, ginagawang mas magastos sa computation ang mga inner product dahil sa mataas na dimensionality.
examples.why: 'nagpapagaling' means heals/improves, the opposite direction of 'makes more expensive'; the trailing 'mas mahal sa computational' still carries the correct cost claim so meaning survives, but the verb choice is wrong and would jar a native technical reader.

examples.source: a feature map that makes your data classes linearly separable
examples.translation: isang feature map na nagpapalit ng linearly separable sa iyong mga klase ng data
examples.suggested: isang feature map na ginagawang linearly separable ang iyong mga klase ng data
examples.why: 'nagpapalit ng linearly separable' (replaces linearly separable) is awkward calque; the intended sense 'makes ... linearly separable' is recoverable but not idiomatic.

editor_note: This is fluent, well-edited Tagalog technical prose that teaches as clearly as the English. Core domain terms (ground state energy, Hamiltonian, ansatz, basis set, expectation value, qubit, superposition, entanglement, cost function, optimizer, convergence) are correctly kept in English and used consistently throughout, which is the right convention for this register; "klasikal" for classical and "enerhiya" for energy read naturally, and the informal register holds with no po/opo slips. All meaning-critical passages are faithful: the variational inequality "mas malaki sa o katumbas ng" (greater-than-or-equal), the upper-bound caveat where measured energy can be "bahagyang mas mababa" than the exact value (line 828), the negative-reaction-energy interpretation "ang energy ay napapalabas... mas mababang energy kaysa sa dalawang hiwalay na atom" (line 1012), and all six True/False polarities and MCQ distractors are preserved correctly. The one genuine defect is line 1018: "Sinuri natin ang variational principle nang pabahay" renders the English "by hand" with "pabahay" (a word relating to housing/dwelling) instead of "nang mano-mano"; it is a clear lexical error but isolated and does not invert or obscure the physics, so it does not mislead the learner.

examples.source: We explored the variational principle by hand, calculating the ground state energy of the simple harmonic oscillator.
examples.translation: Sinuri natin ang variational principle nang pabahay, na kinakalkula ang ground state energy ng simpleng harmonic oscillator.
examples.suggested: Sinuri natin ang variational principle nang mano-mano, na kinakalkula ang ground state energy ng simpleng harmonic oscillator.
examples.why: 'Pabahay' means house-/dwelling-related and is the wrong word for 'by hand' (manual calculation), which should be 'nang mano-mano' or 'sa pamamagitan ng kamay'. Isolated lexical slip; meaning still recoverable, not misleading.

editor_note: This is meaning-faithful, well-edited technical Tagalog that teaches as clearly as the English. Domain terms are handled with the correct convention — qubit, gate, circuit, Hamiltonian, expectation value, observable, spin swap, transpilation, backend, shots, Sampler/Estimator, magnetization are kept in English and bound with native connective prose (ebolusyon for evolution, pagpapalapita/mapalapitan for approximation, akumulasyon for accumulation), all used consistently file-wide. The load-bearing physics passages survive intact: the Trotterization accuracy/depth tradeoff caveat (line 67, "hindi mapapabayaang alalahanin"), the commute-vs-non-commute Taylor-series argument, and especially the dense measurement-outcome paragraph at line 1025 (transversal α=0° → near-certain spin swap; longitudinal α=±90° → no evolution, measure ↓↑ as prepared; α=±45° → all combinations with spin swap most likely at 67%) all match the source with no inverted condition, swapped quantity, or dropped caveat. Register is correctly informal (tayo/natin, no po/opo). The only blemishes are cosmetic and non-misleading: two typos ("msusukat" for masusukat at line 1025, "maunawaang" for maunawaan at line 35), the slightly imprecise "Ang huli sa dalawa" (line 194) for "the latter two [of the three]", and a mildly stiff "interesadong dami" (line 228) for "a quantity of particular interest" — none alter meaning, so no MINOR/FAIL is warranted.

The prose reads as native, well-edited Ukrainian technical writing: domain terminology is correct and fully consistent — гамільтоніан, анзац, кубіт, гейт, схема, бекенд, функція вартості, значення математичного сподівання (expectation value), варіаційні параметри, збіжність, пом'якшення помилок, заплутування — and brand/API names (Estimator, COBYLA, efficient_su2, Qiskit Runtime, BackendEstimator) are correctly kept in English. The informal "ти" register holds throughout (звернись, здогадався(лась), Ти виконав(ла)), with inclusive gendered verb forms. Meaning is faithful paragraph-by-paragraph including the subtle parts (the "no reason to think we found the ground state energy" caveat in Step 4, the highly-entangled 127-qubit claim, the optimization_level low-vs-high contrast — none inverted or softened). The ONE defect: the third bullet under the session-options list has the raw English source sentence "Estimator's built-in resilience: As with optimization..." left in the body (line 263) immediately above its correct Ukrainian translation "Вбудована стійкість Estimator..." (line 264) — a splice/leak artifact that duplicates the bullet and surfaces untranslated English in the rendered page. This is a visible leak, not a meaning inversion, so it is MINOR rather than FAIL; the translated line itself is accurate.

examples.source: * Estimator's built-in resilience: As with optimization, Estimator has built-in settings for resilience against errors, corresponding to different approaches to error mitigation. To learn about resilience level settings, refer to the documentation.
examples.translation: Line 263 retains the raw English bullet verbatim; line 264 then gives the Ukrainian: '* Вбудована стійкість Estimator: Як і з оптимізацією, Estimator має вбудовані налаштування стійкості до помилок...'
examples.suggested: Delete the leaked English line 263; keep only the Ukrainian bullet (line 264).
examples.why: The source has a single bullet; the translation file emits the untranslated English sentence AND its translation, so a reader sees an English paragraph inside otherwise-Ukrainian prose. Meaning is preserved by line 264, but the duplicated English bullet is a visible document defect.

editor_note: This is meaning-faithful, well-edited technical Ukrainian that teaches as clearly as the English. I read paragraph-against-paragraph through the whole file, with extra care on the back half (geometric picture, "Why is Grover useful?", Minesweeper, critical concepts), and found no semantic drift or hallucination: every quantitative claim is intact (O(N), N/2, π√N/4, O(√N), the t≈(π/4)√(N/|A1|)−1/2 formula, the 10%/0.00954 20-qubit argument), the over-rotation caveat is preserved ("якщо ти застосовуєш занадто багато ітерацій, ти оберташся за |A1⟩ і ймовірність... почне знову зменшуватися"), and the delicate odd/even parity reasoning for the Minesweeper "2" cell is rendered correctly ("повертає true, коли непарна кількість... Ми хочемо, щоб парна кількість (конкретно дві)... тому заперечуємо"), as is the result (state 101, mines at x_0 and x_2). Core terminology is the correct conventional domain choice and is used consistently — кубіт, вентиль Адамара, суперпозиція, оракул/чорна скринька, оператор Гровера, перекидання фази, посилення амплітуди, транспілювати, бекенд, примітив Sampler — and the informal "ти" register holds throughout. The only flaws are three mechanical orthographic slips that do not affect meaning: a typo "перевернеце" for "переверне" (L391), the wrong preposition "зі масштабуванням" instead of "з масштабуванням" (L965), and the non-standard "зворотньому" for "зворотному" (L809); these are outside the meaning-drift focus and keep the verdict at PASS.

examples.source: Since XOR is a parity operation... returns true when an *odd* number of the variables are true. We want an *even* number (specifically two) to be true, so we negate with ¬
examples.translation: Оскільки XOR є операцією парності... повертає true, коли *непарна* кількість змінних є true. Ми хочемо, щоб *парна* кількість (конкретно дві) була true, тому заперечуємо з ¬
examples.why: Checked specifically because odd/even and the parenthetical 'specifically two' are easy to invert; the Ukrainian preserves непарна/парна and 'конкретно дві' exactly — no drift. Cited as a verified-clean passage, not a defect.

examples.source: the first X gate will flip qubit 0 to |1⟩
examples.translation: перший X-вентиль перевернеце кубіт 0 на |1⟩
examples.why: Orthographic typo: 'перевернеце' is not a word; intended 'переверне'. Meaning remains clear, so MINOR only — flagged for the record, does not change the PASS verdict.
examples.suggested: перший X-вентиль переверне кубіт 0 на |1⟩

examples.source: Can you see any problems with scaling Grover's algorithm on modern quantum computers?
examples.translation: Чи бачиш ти якісь проблеми зі масштабуванням алгоритму Гровера на сучасних квантових комп'ютерах?
examples.why: Wrong preposition variant 'зі' before 'масштабуванням'; standard Ukrainian takes 'з'. Purely mechanical, no meaning impact.
examples.suggested: Чи бачиш ти якісь проблеми з масштабуванням алгоритму Гровера на сучасних квантових комп'ютерах?

editor_note: This is native, well-edited Ukrainian technical prose that reads as if written by a Ukrainian-speaking quantum specialist, not machine-translated. Core terminology is conventional and consistent: "алгоритм Ґровера", "кубіт", "гейт запиту"/"модель запитів" (query gate/query model), "булева схема" (Boolean circuit), "суперпозиція", "розв'язок" (solution, used consistently), "задача з обіцянкою" (promise problem), "двійковий алфавіт". Meaning is faithful paragraph-by-paragraph including the load-bearing claims — the $O(\sqrt{N})$ vs $O(N)$ complexity contrast, "guarantee success" rendered as "гарантовано знайти відповідь", the "exactly one solution" promise, and the "not a promise problem" negation are all preserved correctly with no inversion, swapped quantity, or dropped caveat. The file uses first-person plural "ми" throughout (mirroring the English "we'll"), so the informal ти/Ви convention does not surface here and there are no register slips. Only cosmetic notes (not defects): "interference patterns" → "інтерференційні картини" is slightly literal versus the more idiomatic singular "інтерференційна картина", and "essentially looking for a needle" → "буквально шукаємо голку" nudges "essentially" toward "literally" — both harmless inside the metaphor. No semantic drift or hallucination found; the translation teaches the unstructured-search setup as clearly as the source.

examples.source: This is described as an *unstructured search* problem... it is not limited to this model; we can run Grover's algorithm on any function $f$ for which we have a Boolean circuit.
examples.translation: Ця задача називається *неструктурованим пошуком*... він не обмежений цією моделлю: алгоритм Ґровера можна запускати для будь-якої функції $f$, для якої є булева схема.
examples.why: Confirms correct, consistent domain terminology (неструктурований пошук, булева схема, модель запитів) and faithful meaning — no defect, cited as evidence of the PASS.

examples.source: this is the best we can do with a deterministic algorithm if we want to guarantee success.
examples.translation: Якщо ми обмежені обчисленням $f$ на обраних вхідних даних і хочемо гарантовано знайти відповідь, це найкраще, що можна зробити детермінованим алгоритмом.
examples.why: The strong claim 'guarantee success' is preserved as 'гарантовано знайти відповідь' with no softening or strengthening; quantities and conditions match the source exactly.
